sys_script.description: Before-insert/update on x_casemgmt_case: when assigned_agent and assigned_group are both populated, validates that the agent is a member of the group via x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.isAgentInGroup(). On failure, surfaces the form-level error 'Assigned agent must be a member of the assigned group.' via gs.addErrorMessage() + current.setAbortAction(true). Per AAP Sections 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 2: Open -> In Progress: 'assigned_agent populated AND member of assigned_group') and 0.5.7 (assigned_agent constraint: 'Optional; must be member of assigned_group'). Order=300 places this rule THIRD in the before-rule chain on x_casemgmt_case, after block_terminal_closed (100) and block_draft_backtransition (200) and before clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress (400) and set_closed_date (500); running after the prohibition guards is a deliberate performance optimization so we don't run the sys_user_grmember GlideRecord lookup on transitions that will be rejected anyway. Dual-triggered (action_insert=true AND action_update=true) because a manager (or seed script) may assign agent and group at case-creation time via a single Table API call, not just through subsequent updates. Skip conditions: (a) either agent or group is empty (b) neither field changed since previous - avoid redundant queries on non-assignment-touching updates. Defensive previous-null guard: on insert previous is null and both change-detection flags evaluate true so the membership is always validated for new rows. Type-agnostic: fires for both General Inquiry and Complaint case types because the membership constraint applies regardless of case type. Defense in depth: validate_inprogress_transition subflow makes the same check at the workflow layer; this BR catches direct DB writes bypassing the flow. The error message wording is NOT prescribed verbatim by AAP Section 0.7.4 (which lists only four verbatim messages); the chosen wording is consistent with the prompt's tone and the AAP's own narrative wording. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id literals (runtime sys_id comparisons via .toString() are platform-canonical and permitted).
sys_script.name: x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership
